Our Work
Projects that move communities forward.

Every project starts with listening. We partner with communities to understand their needs before any engineering begins.

Completed 2009 - 2014

Isongo, Kenya Water Supply Project

Background: EWB-UMBC launched its Kenya program in January 2013, traveling to Isongo, a rural community of approximately 500 people, in partnership with Simiyu House Kenya. The assessment team included undergraduate students, a professional engineering mentor, and a faculty advisor. During the trip, the team tested water quality, mapped the community using GPS, and built relationships with community leaders and local university partners.

Need: Isongo residents relied on a single water source confirmed unsafe for consumption, with elevated nutrient levels from agricultural runoff and dangerous levels of E. coli, total coliforms, and Enterobacteria. The team identified solutions to address both water safety and sanitation needs for the community's 500 residents.

Completed 2017 - 2023

San Rafael Water Supply Project

Background: EWB-UMBC worked in San Rafael de Arancibia in collaboration with the Integral Development Association of San Rafael de Arancibia. The chapter proposed constructing a new break-pressure tank with a chlorinator and an upgraded piping system. A 2018 assessment trip evaluated existing infrastructure and identified additional needs.

Need: The existing pipe system left six households without any aqueduct connection and the broader community without reliable water during the dry season. The team installed a new storage tank and chlorinator, new transmission pipes from both water sources, distribution pipes for the six unconnected homes, and protective infrastructure for an exposed water source.

Ongoing 2021 - Present

Habitat for Humanity Builds

EWB-UMBC partners with Habitat for Humanity on residential construction projects across Maryland. Members volunteer on build days to help families move closer to homeownership.

  • 12+ families helped over the past 5 years
  • Multiple build sites across Maryland
  • Ongoing partnership - new builds each year
